Global (All).title: US-China Relations: Tensions Escalate Over Taiwan
Global (All).summary: Recent military exercises by the US and China have heightened tensions over Taiwan, leading to concerns about the potential for conflict.

Global (All).title: Global Food Crisis: Prices Soar Due to Supply Chain Disruptions
Global (All).summary: Global food prices have skyrocketed due to supply chain disruptions, trade restrictions, and adverse weather conditions, sparking food shortages and social unrest.

Global (All).title: AI Regulation: Governments Struggle to Keep Pace with Advancing Technology
Global (All).summary: The rapid development of artificial intelligence (AI) has left governments scrambling to regulate its use, leading to concerns about data privacy, job displacement, and national security.

United States.summary: Supreme Court overturns Roe v. Wade, sparking protests
Texas school shooting: 19 children and 2 adults killed in Uvalde
US Capitol riot: FBI identifies key suspect, Timothy Hale-Cusanelli, in storming of Senate Chamber

South Korea.title: Rallies in South Korea Protest Military Conscription
South Korea.summary: Thousands of South Koreans took to the streets in Seoul and other cities to protest the country's military conscription system and the government's plans to extend the service period. The protests were largely peaceful, but some turned violent, with police using tear gas to disperse the crowds.

South Korea.title: South Korea's President Embroiled in Corruption Scandal
South Korea.summary: South Korea's President Yoon Suk-yeol is facing growing calls for his resignation after his daughter was implicated in a corruption scandal involving a lucrative business deal. The scandal has sparked widespread outrage and protests across the country.

South Korea.title: Anti-Thunder-700 Missile System Protests Continue in South Korea
South Korea.summary: Protests against the deployment of the US-made THAAD (Terminal High Altitude Area Defense) missile defense system have continued in several South Korean cities, despite the government's reassurances that the system is necessary to protect the country from North Korean threats.
